item.title: Barney Frank, US congressman, 1940-2026
item.description: First openly gay member of Congress led landmark banking reforms in wake of the financial crisis

item.title: Celebrate your looming obsolescence with our new ‘lower-value human capital’ merch
item.description: Because you’re not worth it

item.title: Judge points to son’s money ‘obsession’ in Mango founder death case
item.description: Son of fashion billionaire Isak Andic denies wrongdoing over father’s 100-metre fall on hiking trip

item.title: Italy’s biggest bank explores bid for Spanish wealth manager
item.description: Intesa Sanpaolo prepares formal offer as Warburg Pincus seeks sale of Singular Bank

item.title: Airbus open to two-fighter jet solution amid impasse with France’s Dassault
item.description: France and Germany remain locked in talks to salvage €100bn project plagued by power struggles between the two groups

item.title: The oil shock is coming for America
item.description: Tools that worked to lower prices in the last crisis have already been spent

item.title: ‘Lazy scapegoating’: supermarket price cap plan prompts furious backlash
item.description: Treasury’s effort to get UK retailers to agree on voluntary price controls struggles to find any support

item.title: Swiss finance faces some awkward questions
item.description: Activists have triggered a vote on becoming a ‘more sustainable financial centre’

item.title: How can we ensure our children inherit equal amounts?
item.description: My wife and I have children from previous marriages and worry about what will happen once one of us dies

item.title: Camping tech to conquer the great outdoors
item.description: Essential kit – from flasks to flashlights

item.title: FirstFT: Trump critic Thomas Massie loses Kentucky primary
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Europe’s largest property deal in years collapses and Apollo’s Marc Rowan accused of improperly using company resources

item.title: Three supertankers attempt Hormuz crossing
item.description: Vessels bound for China and South Korea are carrying 6mn barrels of crude oil

item.title: Indonesia is at a perpendicular intersection of two different roads
item.description: Is this the worst ageing fuel subsidy in the world?

item.title: UK eases sanctions on Russian oil refined into jet fuel and diesel
item.description: Government aims to alleviate supply problems caused by Iran war and echoes similar measures in US

item.title: StanChart does damage control after boss calls staff ‘lower-value human capital’
item.description: Bill Winters says cutting almost 8,000 jobs reflects changing work practices and not ‘value of our people’

item.title: China banned Nvidia’s gaming chip during Jensen Huang’s visit
item.description: Beijing aims to support domestic players including Huawei and Cambricon as they catch up to US rivals

item.title: Europe’s biggest office deal since 2022 collapses
item.description: Buyer fails to raise funds for €850mn acquisition of Frankfurt’s OpernTurm from JPMorgan and GIC

item.title: Marks and Spencer forecasts return to profit growth after cyber attack
item.description: Retailer stages recovery from attack that cost it hundreds of millions of pounds

item.title: Euro stablecoin project gains backing of 37 banks
item.description: Dutch company Qivalis adds 25 new lenders as continent seeks to challenge dollar’s crypto dominance

item.title: Stellantis strikes deal to build Dongfeng cars in France
item.description: Jeep and Peugeot owner to make Chinese group’s high-end vehicles at underutilised Rennes plant

item.title: Reeves to set out plan to curb legal challenges to ‘critical’ green energy projects
item.description: Move to boost Britain’s energy independence would grant parliament powers to speed up sluggish planning process

item.title: Is Nvidia too big to fail?
item.description: ‘You’re clearly at the centre of everything’

item.title: How AI turbocharged Trump’s ‘slopaganda’ machine
item.description: US president’s stream of fake imagery on Truth Social is reshaping the boundaries of political communication

item.title: Global EV and hybrid sales to hit 30% of car market this year, IEA forecasts
item.description: Driver response to rising fuel prices and lower vehicle costs expected to propel sales

item.title: AI Exchange
item.description: In this monthly series, running alongside our existing Tech Exchange dialogues, FT journalists talk to the scientists, developers and business leaders exploring ever more applications for artificial intelligence, in every aspect of our lives

item.title: Introducing: The Story of Money
item.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

item.title: To the moon: SpaceX’s generational payday
item.description: Who’s positioned for a windfall from the listing, and why it’s historic in more ways than one

item.title: Marc Rowan accused of misusing Apollo resources for political work
item.description: Chief executive of private capital group used company email and staff for lobbying, unions say

item.title: Merger spirits could lift the UK’s languishing mid-caps
item.description: S&P 400 index makes it well suited to active investing — and opportunistic dealmaking

item.title: UK housebuilders suffer £8bn hit from Iran war
item.description: Share prices have fallen sharply as hedge funds bet the industry will bear the brunt of the economic fallout
